meta.title: What’s your holiday mode this year?
meta.author: Qzera
meta.description: A short three-question holiday mini-experience that shows which Christmas mode you’re in this year.

Q2_A.text:
You pause for a moment

When your day starts to fall apart a bit, you’d rather slow down: you sit down with a tea, light a candle, and let the warm light pull you back into a holiday mood.

Q2_B.text:
You look for a bit of festive boost

When you’re getting tired, you turn things up: you start a Christmas playlist, switch on the fairy lights, or come up with a spontaneous plan to get the energy going again.

Q2_C.text:
You go back to a trusted ritual

When the day wobbles, you reach for a familiar tradition: a recipe, a favorite movie, or an evening walk among the lights that helps you reset the mood.

Q2_D.text:
You give yourself a quiet break

When too many things are happening at once, you’d rather step out for a while: you turn down the noise, retreat for a few minutes, and let the holiday day settle inside you.

pages.title: How does the holiday season start for you?

text.text: Pick the kind of start that feels closest to you.

choices.text: In the morning I light a candle and tidy up.
Messages, planning and meetups start from the morning.
I take out my favorite mug and a classic movie.
First there’s running around, then quiet by the lights.

pages.title: What brings you back into a holiday mood?

text.text: When you’re a bit out of it, what brings you back into the festive feeling most reliably?

choices.text: I sit down with a hot tea next to the lights.
I put on some Christmas music.
I step outside into the cold air for a few minutes.

pages.title: What shapes your Christmas mood the most this year?

text.text: By the end of the day, what defines your holiday mood the most?

choices.text: When there’s finally calm in the home.
When there are quiet, intimate moments.
When by the end of the day everything has smoothed out.

text.text: Based on your answers, we’ll show you which holiday mode you’re in this year.

text.default: This year your holidays run in a calm, structured mode. It matters to you that you can see the shape of the day: space to breathe, structure, and a few quieter stretches where you can really be present. You’re not chasing maximum program density – you want to experience Christmas in your own tempo, comfortably.

text.default: This year your Christmas leans into a more social, energetic mode. You feel good when things are happening: meetups, shared programs, spontaneous ideas. What gives you energy is when the holidays aren’t static, but alive and moving, filled with smaller and bigger experiences.

text.default: For you, the heart of this Christmas is connection and depth: it’s not about quantity, but about having one or two moments that truly stay with you. It might be a conversation, a shared dinner, or a small gesture — what matters is that it feels honest, and there’s real space in it for everyone.
